android.widget.TextView.text: HRW Accuses Israel of Using White Phosphorous Over Lebanese Homes
android.widget.TextView.heading: false

android.widget.TextView.text: On March 9, Human Rights Watch (HRW) accused Israel of unlawfully using white phosphorus over residential parts of a southern Lebanese town last week, citing the March 3 incident.
android.widget.TextView.heading: false

android.widget.TextView.text: Amid the escalation, the Israeli military ordered evacuations south of the Litani River and in Yohmor and 50 other villages on March 3, according to authorities.
android.widget.TextView.heading: false

android.widget.TextView.text: Verified images from March 3 showed seven airburst white phosphorus munitions and civil defense workers extinguishing fires in two homes and one car, consistent with M825-series 155mm artillery projectiles, HRW said.
android.widget.TextView.heading: false

android.widget.TextView.text: HRW urged Israel to prohibit airburst white phosphorus use in populated areas and called on states supplying Israel with weapons to suspend military assistance and arms sales.
android.widget.TextView.heading: false
